items.inputUnderTest: median(arr): return the median of a numeric array (average the two middle values for even length).
items.referenceImpl: export function median(arr){const s=[...arr].sort((a,b)=>a-b);const n=s.length;return n%2?s[(n-1)/2]:(s[n/2-1]+s[n/2])/2;}
requirements: odd-length returns the middle element
even-length averages the two middle elements
a single-element array returns that element
unsorted input is handled

items.inputUnderTest: rangeOverlap(a1,a2,b1,b2): do the inclusive ranges [a1,a2] and [b1,b2] overlap?
requirements: clearly overlapping ranges return true
disjoint ranges return false
ranges touching at exactly one endpoint return true (inclusive)
one range fully inside another returns true

items.inputUnderTest: truncateWords(s,n): keep the first n words; append '...' only if the string had MORE than n words.
requirements: fewer than n words returns the string unchanged
exactly n words returns unchanged with NO ellipsis
more than n words truncates and appends the ellipsis
the ellipsis is only added when actually truncated

items.inputUnderTest: isLeapYear(y): Gregorian leap year — divisible by 4, except centuries which must be divisible by 400.
requirements: a year divisible by 4 (non-century) is a leap year
a year not divisible by 4 is not
a century year NOT divisible by 400 is NOT a leap year
a century year divisible by 400 IS a leap year

items.inputUnderTest: classifyNumber(n): return 'neg' if n<0, 'zero' if n===0, else 'pos'.
requirements: a negative number returns 'neg'
zero returns 'zero'
a positive number returns 'pos'
the zero boundary is distinct from positive
